title: The Regulatory Ceasefire Is Over
gist: Paywall teaser: China's regulatory enforcers are back in action — fining brokerages Tiger and Futu over offshore money flows, blocking Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, fining internet platforms over food delivery, and probing Trip.com — reviving memories of the 2021 'regulatory storm' before the post-2022 reassurance pivot. Light card — intro only.

insights.claim: In late May 2026 China's stock-market regulators fined and punished online brokerages Tiger and Futu over moving money offshore and announced a broader crackdown on cross-border securities trading.
insights.speaker: Christopher Beddor, Tilly Zhang
insights.so_what: Confirms a renewed enforcement posture; direct downside for offshore brokerages dependent on mainland flow.
insights.quote: stock-market regulators fined and punished the online brokerages Tiger and Futu for their involvement in moving money offshore, and announced a broader crackdown on cross-border securities trading

insights.claim: Chinese agencies have this year blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, fined major internet platforms over food-delivery irregularities, and opened an antitrust probe into online travel service Trip.com.
insights.speaker: Christopher Beddor, Tilly Zhang
insights.so_what: Broad-based actions across deals, platforms and travel signal sector-wide regulatory risk for Chinese internet equities.
insights.quote: blocked Meta’s acquisition of the artificial-intelligence startup Manus, fined the major internet platforms for alleged irregularities in their food-delivery operations, and announced an antitrust probe into online travel service Trip.com

insights.claim: The current flurry of Chinese regulatory activity revives memories of the 2021 'regulatory storm,' which broadened from internet platforms to finance, property and education before the government pivoted to a reassuring, growth-stabilizing tone after markets slumped in 2022.
insights.speaker: Christopher Beddor, Tilly Zhang
insights.so_what: The 2021-22 analog is the template — watch whether enforcement broadens across sectors and how quickly Beijing reverses if markets fall.
insights.quote: This flurry of activity has revived memories of the infamous “regulatory storm” of 2021
